mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending corporate revenue contraction, causing investors to discount equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in auto sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving upward revisions to corporate earnings estimates and lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a late-cycle environment indicates firms are utilizing excess capacity to meet demand, expanding profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while SPX forward returns remain negative for > 50% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient drops below zero in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the consumer price index reduces inflation expectations and forces the Federal Reserve to pause rate hikes, easing financial conditions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline following disinflationary prints, or the forward return differential between disinflationary and inflationary months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market that sustains consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows negative or insignificant coefficients in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are not higher when both conditions align versus when only one aligns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly scales aggregate corporate revenues, causing analysts to raise earnings forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the rolling correlation between GDP surprises and equity returns remains below 0.1 over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, a simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate amplifies recession risk pricing, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both high rates and rising unemployment coincide, or the interaction effect vanishes in a walk-forward test spanning multiple rate cycles.
